feature: Add Diet Type and Meals Per Day to Edit Profile modal in daily-view

prompts.description: Add Diet Type and Meals Per Day panels to the Edit Profile modal in daily-view.tsx
prompts.prompt:
In components/daily-view.tsx, find the showEditProfileModal section (around line 770). It has three settings-section-panel blocks: 'Basics', 'Body stats', and 'Weekly context'. After the 'Weekly context' panel's closing tag and before the Save/Cancel buttons, add two new settings-section-panel blocks:

Panel A — Diet Type:
- Header dot + label: 'Diet type'
- Description: 'Controls the type of meals generated in your plan.'
- Read components/onboarding.tsx to find the dietTypes array and reuse the exact same values and labels
- Render as choice cards using the same pattern as settings-screen.tsx Goal/Activity panels
- selected = editDietType state variable (check if it exists in daily-view.tsx; if not, add useState initialized to userProfile.dietType)
- onClick: setEditDietType(item.id)
- Use settings-choice-card, settings-choice-card-active, settings-choice-card-idle classes

Panel B — Meals Per Day:
- Header dot + label: 'Meals per day'
- Description: 'How many meals to split your daily calories across.'
- Render [3, 4, 5] as a flex row of buttons
- selected = editMealsPerDay state variable (check if it exists; if not, add useState initialized to userProfile.mealsPerDay)
- onClick: setEditMealsPerDay(n)
- Style: settings-chip + settings-chip-active/idle classes, flex-1

Also ensure that when the Save button is clicked, editDietType and editMealsPerDay are included in the setUserProfile call alongside the existing fields. Find the save handler in daily-view.tsx and add these two fields.

Do not change any other logic.
